source_paper_title: A Single-Loop Algorithm for Decentralized Bilevel Optimization
context:
Page 4, Section 1 (Introduction, paragraph after the list of heterogeneity assumptions)

Extending the algorithms in this paper to stochastic DBO is definitely another very important task, and we leave it to a future work.
title: Design a provably convergent single-loop algorithm for stochastic decentralized bilevel optimization without heterogeneity assumptions
latex:
Given an integer \(n\ge 1\), let \(F_i:\mathbb{R}^p\times\mathbb{R}^q\to\mathbb{R}\) and \(f_i:\mathbb{R}^p\times\mathbb{R}^q\to\mathbb{R}\) be (possibly stochastic) loss functions associated with agent \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\). Let \(\xi_i\) denote a random variable local to agent \(i\), and define population objectives
\[
F(x,y)=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \mathbb{E}_{\xi_i}\big[ F_i(x,y;\xi_i)\big],\qquad
f(x,y)=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \mathbb{E}_{\xi_i}\big[ f_i(x,y;\xi_i)\big].
\]
Assume that for every \(x\in\mathbb{R}^p\), the function \(y\mapsto f(x,y)\) is \(\sigma\)-strongly convex for some \(\sigma>0\), so the lower-level optimizer
\[
y^*(x)=\arg\min_{y\in\mathbb{R}^q} f(x,y)
\]
is well-defined and unique. Consider the decentralized bilevel optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^p}\ \Phi(x):=F\big(x,y^*(x)\big).
\]
Agents communicate over a fixed connected network encoded by a symmetric doubly stochastic mixing matrix \(W\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) with spectral gap \(1-\rho>0\), where \(\rho:=\max\{|\lambda_2(W)|,|\lambda_n(W)|\}<1\).

Problem: Design a decentralized, fully single-loop, first-order stochastic algorithm that (i) uses only stochastic gradient and Hessian-vector/Jacobian-vector products computed from minibatches of \(\xi_i\) at each agent and \(\Theta(1)\) communication rounds per iteration; (ii) does not assume any gradient or data heterogeneity bounds across agents (i.e., no uniform bound on \(\|\nabla f_i-\nabla f\|\) or similar); and (iii) achieves a nonasymptotic convergence guarantee to stationarity, for example a bound of the form
\[
\min_{0\le k\le K-1} \mathbb{E}\big[\|\nabla \Phi(\bar x_k)\|^2\big] \le \frac{C}{K}
\]
for some constant \(C\) independent of \(K\), where \(\bar x_k:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i^k\) is the network average iterate.
